Okay, welcome back. Now it's time to build the campaign. Now look, this is going to be really hard to resist the urge to overcomplicate this. All right, so please don't overcomplicate it. Like, this is really, really, really simple and it's super simple for a reason and we've already talked about those reasons like nine million times. Okay, so what we want to do is we're going to build out our first two level one campaigns. All right, and I'm going to start with the one that's super broad. So I'm going to just click on create a campaign, big green button. You always want to make sure this is checked. All right, and so if you're new to this stuff, like, there's all these different objectives. Remember, I said, hey, there's a billion things that we can do with Facebook and all of these ad platforms in terms of telling them how to get us customers. Right now, we're just going for conversions. So click that. All right, don't have to do anything. Then click on continue. All right, and now we're going to name the campaign. Okay, so I'm going to name this one. Let's see. RMS stands for Rainmaker School. Actually, I won't put that in there. I'm just going to put this for course, super broad demo. 816. So that's for August 16. So this first campaign that I'm going to build is going to be the one where we're just choosing gigantic audiences. All right, with really no targeting at all. And so all I've done here is I've just named the campaign. You can name it anything you want to. All right, I just like to name stuff in a way that I can sort of look at the campaign and understand what's going on. So that's real simple. What we do want to make sure is that this is set of conversions, which it is, we don't need to mess with any of this stuff. All right, we want to leave this off. This is important. And we want to leave this off. So that was pretty easy. All right, so now we're going to click on next. Okay. And now we're at the ad set level. All right, so if you refer back to that diagram that I made you earlier, like this is where we choose the audience. So all we're doing here is we're choosing who we're showing the ad to. And because this is super broad, I'm going to start with all US age 34 to 55. Okay, and so this is literally every human in the United States between the ages of 34 and 55. Now, it's always important to think about your long term goal. All right, so what I'm going to be doing here is I'm going to be selling my $4 bundle. And that's got some upsells and stuff. And if I go like to other countries, and I do worldwide and all of that kind of stuff, I know I can make an immediate profit on that. But really my long term goal here is to get customers that really like my stuff and that'll buy things from me later. And a lot of times that involves having to talk to somebody on my team. So I'm going to, I'm going to like put a lot of attention into targeting US based people right now. And maybe some other countries too, or it's easier for us to get on the phone as opposed to global and worldwide. All right, so if you're, and the way this translates to you directly, is if you're selling something that really doesn't matter where they live, then you want to, you definitely want an ad set where you're targeting every single human on the planet over the age of 18. If you're an ideal person, you know, in the long term as someone that you can communicate with, you know, the time zone is not going to be an issue. And they're typically going to have more money or whatever, you might want to choose an age group and the specific country. Okay, so that's why I'm choosing that. So you probably only have one pixel in your Facebook ads manager naturally, I have a ton because I've done a lot of experiments. I'm going to use this one. Okay, and for how to set up tracking pixels and tracking and all that kind of stuff, just defer to our tutorials on how to do that. And this is going to be for a purchase. So I'm telling Facebook to get me a purchase. And this is the pixel that I want you to talk to. I want you to like talk to this particular pixel in my ad account. All right. And again, if you don't know what the hell I'm talking about with pixels and stuff, please look at our tutorials and how to set up the pixel part and the tracking part of your, your ads account. All right. But I'm going to assume you've got that all set up here. Now this is set off by default. That's because we're not going to do it. Dynamic creative means we can have like 10 different pictures and 10 different headlines and 10 different body texts and all that stuff and split test them all at once in a million times. We're not going to do that. We're also going to leave this off, which is great. So now we just want to select the budget. I'm going to choose five bucks. All right. And I could get more data if I did more money, but I'm just going to choose five. Now notice it says add me, add me get zero purchases. You're going to see warnings like that. And it's trying to show a warning over here. You're going to see warnings all the time when you're setting up your account and you're setting up your ad campaigns. Just ignore them. It's Facebook trying to get you to spend more money. All right. So that's all that we have to worry about here. Down here, we'll deal with this stuff later, but this is where I could potentially choose like audiences that I've already created and saved or retargeting audiences. All I really care about right now is I've got United States selected and I'm going to update my age and my age is going to be from 34. All right. To 55. And I'm kind of like guessing in terms of the 55 limit here, but I know usually, generally speaking, people under 34, they don't really invest in higher ticket stuff like this training. Right. So I don't really care about the $4 thing. I just want to get a customer who goes through my stuff and likes it and gets results. And then I can sell them this training later. So I know like an 18 year old kid or something, probably not. That 18 year old kid might buy my $4 thing and they might make a quick profit on the front end, but I'm setting it up this way with the long term thinking. Okay. So detailed targeting. This is where I could choose. All right. Everybody who's interested in Gary Vee or whatever right now, I'm going to leave this alone and I'm going to uncheck this thing. You always got to watch this. Basically, this is Facebook going, Hey, do you want us to show this to people that you're not telling us to show it to? And sometimes that's useful, but right now it's not. So I'm going to uncheck it. And because this is targeting everyone in the United States, the primary language of the United States is English, I don't really need to mess with this. Okay. So this part is now done. This is where we could choose to show it to people, you know, only on Instagram or only on certain placements. We don't do that. The reason we don't do that is because we know that certain placements don't work typically like Facebook marketplace and they're not their news feed, but their content network thingy and their instant articles, they don't usually convert. Facebook's algorithm knows that too. And it's by default going to show it in the right areas. So if we try to filter it and say, no, only show it in these certain areas, then we're just going to end up spending more money when in reality, Facebook wouldn't spend any money in those areas anyway. So this gets us cheaper placements in the auction here. The less we try to filter Facebook, the better of a cost we get. And we're not going to mess with any of this stuff. Right. So super basic. We just literally named it, told it what pixel and what event to optimize for. And again, if you don't know how to do that, defer to our trainings on how to do that stuff. And then I got my daily budgets, five bucks. That's cool. All right. And everybody in the United States, 34 to 55. That's it. Okay. So now I click on next. And now it's time to create an ad. And of course, like, look, we've got like all this stuff, you know, everything's doomed and you've done it wrong. So before I create these things, I usually have a little notepad open, which I've gotten now. And it's got all the copy in the URL on it, because it makes my life easier. That's the one under the picture. I've got that already written. I got my tracking parameters already set here. All right. So I got everything good to go. And now it's going to give me some dummy stuff right here. It's going to start showing me what my ad looks like. I'm also, I'm using an app called Hyros to track my ads. And you can learn about them by just going to YouTube and typing in H-Y-R-O-S. If you want to see tutorials on that stuff, it's really cool. And so because of that, all right, I'm going to use a tracking parameter. And if you want to use tracking parameters and get fancy with tracking, just watch our tutorials on how to do that. But this will tell that particular app, hey, this is where those sales came from. And so when you see me pull up that black interface that we've looked at a couple of times, it has my stats, it's Hyros basically just measuring my Facebook revenue for me. Because Facebook's reporting can kind of suck sometimes. So that's cool. All right. So now I got, you know, I'm telling Facebook where to send the traffic. I got this tracking URL parameter down here, which is not, you know, mandatory. This is something that I like to do because I'm a tracking freak. And Hyros works pretty good. So now I'm going to put in my primary text, all right. And I'm going to put that right here. So I'm just going to paste it. And this is what goes above your picture. Now, here's the deal. You always want like even, which I hope you don't do now, but when we start writing longer copy, you want the thing that they see, you know, like if we write longer copy, they have to click learn more and it'll expand this and there will be longer copy. You want that first sentence that a prospect sees in their news feed to make sense, right? So in this case, I'm not writing longer copy. It's literally get four marketing courses from Frank Kern for $4. Why? Well, I'm targeting every freaking person in the United States over the age of 34 and under the age of 55. So I want Facebook's algorithm to know who I'm looking for. So if people click this damn thing, then chances are they're only, they're clicking it because they're interested in buying marketing courses, right? They're not interested in it because it's got my name. Like this audience is what, 27 million people or something like that or something bigger than that. I don't know. It's a ton. So those people don't know who the hell I am. So like, you know, if I was selling a Facebook course or something, I would click like read this or get this course if you want to make Facebook ads work. If this is going straight to a sales letter about a Facebook course, if this was going to a free report about making Facebook ads work, I would say something like read this if you want to make Facebook ads work. So I call that dog whistle copy like really, really obvious copy and I'll put the, I'll put like a little template of the sentence below this video in your members area so you can just use it when you're writing your own copy. Okay, but this is number like this is it. This is the whole thing for our primary text. We're not writing the freaking novella. I'm going to have the same thing as my quote unquote headline, which really doesn't make any sense because your headline goes under the dang picture, which is the weirdest thing ever. So this says for complete marketing courses for $4. We don't know for sure that they really do. Facebook just thinks they do. So what we want to do is we want to start training Facebook who we want and we're using this unbelievably obvious copy to start doing that and that's freaking important. Okay. So you might be wondering, well, how long should you leave this test in the next one? We're about to make the next video. How long should you leave these running? And the answer is for however long it'll, it takes you to spend whatever you're willing to get your first result. So I'm going to leave this like I'm willing to spend, let's just say $25 to get a customer. So I'm willing to spend 25 bucks to get one. So I'm going to let this thing run until each one of these guys have spent $25. All right. And if it's getting sales at $25, then great. I just wanted to show you that just from using the suggestions thing, just like arbitrarily picking stuff, every single one of these things, logically speaking, every single one of these audiences should contain people who would be interested in buying stuff like what I'm selling. So there's a gazillion people in the world that like 303 million, a lot of them that like e-commerce. Right? So I'll copy that and then just put it in my ad name or ad set name rather. And we're good to go. Now, there's one thing I want, I want you to pay special attention to. All right. So if we look over here, we have all of these guys are in draft. Okay. They're all turned on, but they're in draft. What I want to do is I just want to publish one of them. And I want to let that one get approved. And then I want to publish the rest. So I could come over here and hit review and publish. And it will like publish all of these. But I'm just going to publish one. And the reason I'm going to do that, whoops, I'm going to do that. Hang on, let me click here, edit. And I'm just going to hit publish. The reason I'm going to do that is I'm just publishing this once. I was publishing the campaign, I was publishing that one ad set, and I was publishing that one ad. The rest of these guys are all still in draft. All right. AdWords is now in review. The reason I've done this is because if for some reason this ad gets declined, which it probably won't, I mean there's only two sentences, but if for some reason one of them gets declined, it's likely that all of these ad sets will get declined. And I would rather have one be declined than 20. So what I'm going to do is I'm going to wait. Then as soon as this guy's approved, then I'm just going to hit publish on the rest of these. And because, you know, the logic of it, all right, if they approve one of them, they'll approve the rest of them. And we should be fine. But that's why I'm doing it that way. All right. So I'm going to end this week here. Now, here is your mission. That is to do what I just did. So it literally took me 45 minutes, you know, to set this whole thing up. When I was off camera and everything, so it wasn't a big deal. I even returned some texts while I was doing it. So it's not that hard. I want you to make these two, I call them seed campaigns. And then I want you to not mess with it. All right. So give it until we meet in the next module. Because you need to let each one get enough, have enough budget thrown at it to where it can get some statistically relevant results. So in my case, I'm willing to spend $25 to get a customer. So I'm going to let each one of these dang things run until they've spent 25 bucks. And then I'm going to scale it. All right. And so we'll show we'll deal with the winners and losers next week. But for now, we've got our seeds made. And are we going to do more advanced stuff than this? Hell, yes, we are. All right. But this is the foundation of every single one of these. And really, what I really should be doing is I should be making like even more ad groups in here with guru names and stuff like this should actually have 200 ad sets. Because if you look at this, this is going to cost me $100 a day. And then the other one we built is going to cost what $20 today. So I'm literally for five days, I'm going to spend $120 a day. That's a very, very small test. All right. So if you want to build more than by all means build more, I suggest five bucks is your daily spend per audience here, regardless of how many you do. It just seems to work well at that level of spend. All right. So get out there and get to it, make your first campaign, and then we'll start scaling. All right. Anxious to see what happens.
